 And where the ten are, there shal never lack a man among them
 who shal study the Law continualy, day and night, concerning the right
 conduct of a man with his companion. And the Congregation shal
 watch in community for a third of every night of the year, to read the
 Book and to study the Law and to bless together.
 This is the Rule for an Assembly of the Congregation
 Each man shal sit in his place: the Priests shal sit first, and the
 elders second, and al the rest of the people according to their rank.
 And thus shal they be questioned concerning the Law, and concerning
 any counsel or matter coming before the Congregation, each man
 bringing his knowledge to the Council of the Community.
 No man shal interrupt a companion before his speech has ended,
 nor speak before a man of higher rank; each man shal speak in his
 turn. And in an Assembly of the Congregation no man shal speak
 without the consent of the Congregation, nor indeed of the Guardian of
 the Congregation. Should any man wish to speak to the Congregation,
 yet not be in a position to question the Council of the Community, let
 him rise to his feet and say: ‘I have something to say to the
 Congregation.’ If they command him to speak, he shal speak.
 Every man, born of Israel, who freely pledges himself to join the
 Council of the Community shal be examined by the Guardian at the
 head of the Congregation concerning his understanding and his
 deeds. If he is fitted to the discipline, he shal admit him into the
 Covenant that he may be converted to the truth and depart from al
 injustice; and he shal instruct him in al the rules of the Community. And
 later, when he comes to stand before the Congregation, they shal al
 deliberate his case, and according to the decision of the Council of the
 Congregation he shal either enter or depart. After he has entered the
 Council of the Community he shal not touch the pure Meal of the
 Congregation until one {ful} (4Q256) year is completed, and until he
 has been examined concerning his spirit and deeds; nor shal he have
 any share of the property of the Congregation. Then when he has
completed one year within the Community, the Congregation shal
 deliberate his case with regard to his understanding and observance
 of the Law. And if it be his destiny, according to the judgement of the
 Priests and the multitude of the men of their Covenant, to enter the
 company of the Community, his property and earnings shal be handed
 over to the Bursar of the Congregation who shal register it to his
 account and shal not spend it for the Congregation. He shal not touch
 the Drink of the Congregation until he has completed a second year
 among the men of the Community. But when the second year has
 passed, he shal be examined, and if it be his destiny, according to the
 judgement of the Congregation, to enter the Community, then he shal
 be inscribed among his brethren in the order of his rank for the Law,
 and for justice, and for the pure Meal; his property shal be merged and
 he shal offer his counsel and judgement to the Community.
 These are the Rules by which they shall judge at a Community
 (Court of) Inquiry according to the cases
 If one of them has lied deliberately in matters of property, he shal be
 excluded from the pure Meal of the Congregation for one year and
 shal do penance with respect to one quarter of his food.
 Whoever has answered his companion with obstinacy, or has
 addressed him impatiently, going so far as to take no account of the
 dignity of his felow by disobeying the order of a brother inscribed
 before him, he has taken the law into his own hand; therefore he shal
 do penance for one year [and shal be excluded].
 If any man has uttered the [Most] Venerable Name VI even though
 frivolously, or as a result of shock or for any other reason whatever,
 while reading the Book or blessing, he shal be dismissed and shal
 return to the Council of the Community no more.
 If he has spoken in anger against one of the Priests inscribed in the
 Book, he shal do penance for one year and shal be excluded for his
 soul’s sake from the pure Meal of the Congregation. But if he has
 spoken unwittingly, he shal do penance for six months.
 Whoever has deliberately lied shal do penance for six months.
Whoever has deliberately insulted his companion unjustly shal do
 penance for one year and shal be excluded.
 Whoever has deliberately deceived his companion by word or by
 deed shal do penance for six months.
 If he has failed to care for his companion, he shal do penance for
 three months. But if he has failed to care for the property of the
 Community, thereby causing its loss, he shal restore it in ful. And if he
 be unable to restore it, he shal do penance for sixty days.
 Whoever has borne malice against his companion unjustly shal do
 penance for six months/one year; and likewise, whoever has taken
 revenge in any matter whatever.
 Whoever has spoken foolishly: three months.
 Whoever has interrupted his companion whilst speaking: ten days.
 Whoever has lain down to sleep during an Assembly of the
 Congregation : thirty days. And likewise, whoever has left, without
 reason, an Assembly of the Congregation as many as three times
 during one Assembly, shal do penance for ten days. But if he has
 departed whilst they were standing he shal do penance for thirty days.
 Whoever has gone naked before his companion, without having
 been obliged to do so, he shal do penance for six months.
 Whoever has spat in an Assembly of the Congregation shal do
 penance for thirty days.
 Whoever has been so poorly dressed that when drawing his hand
 from beneath his garment his nakedness has been seen, he shal do
 penance for thirty days.
 Whoever has guffawed foolishly shal do penance for thirty days.
 Whoever has drawn out his left hand to gesticulate with it shal do
 penance for ten days.
 Whoever has gone about slandering his companion shal be
 excluded from the pure Meal of the Congregation for one year and
 shal do penance. But whoever has slandered the Congregation shal
 be expeled from among them and shal return no more.
 Whoever has murmured against the authority of the Community shal
 be expeled and shal not return. But if he has murmured against his
 companion unjustly, he shal do penance for six months.
 Should a man return whose spirit has so trembled before the
authority of the Community that he has betrayed the truth and walked in
 the stubbornness of his heart, he shal do penance for two years.
 During the first year he shal not touch the pure Meal of the
 Congregation, and during the second year he shal not touch the Drink
 of the Congregation and shal sit below al the men of the Community.
 Then when his two years are completed, the Congregation shal
 consider his case, and if he is admitted he shal be inscribed in his
 rank and may then question concerning the Law.
 If, after being in the Council of the Community for ten ful years, the
 spirit of any man has failed, so that he has betrayed the Community
 and departed from the Congregation to walk in the stubbornness of his
 heart, he shal return no more to the Council of the Community.
 Moreover, if any member of the Community has shared with him his
 food or property which ... of the Congregation, his sentence shal be
 the same; he shal be ex[peled].
 V I In the Council of the Community there shal be twelve men and
 three Priests, perfectly versed in a l that is revealed of the Law, whose
 works shal be truth, righteousness, justice, loving-kindness and
 humility. They shal preserve the faith in the Land with steadfastness
 and meekness and shal atone for sin by the practice of justice and by
 suffering the sorrows of affliction. They shal walk with al men
 according to the standard of truth and the rule of the time.
 When these are in Israel, the Council of the Community shal be
 established in truth. It sha l be an Everlasting Plantation, a House of
 Holiness for Israel, an Assembly of Supreme Holiness for Aaron. They
 shal be witnesses to the truth at the Judgement, and shal be the elect
 of Goodwil who shal atone for the Land and pay to the wicked their
 reward. It shal be that tried wal, that precious corner-stone, whose
 foundations shal neither rock nor sway in their place (Isa. xxviii, 16). It
 shal be a Most Holy Dweling for Aaron, with everlasting knowledge of
 the Covenant of justice, and shal offer up sweet fragrance. It shal be a
 House of Perfection and Truth in Israel that they may establish a
 Covenant according to the everlasting precepts. And they shal be an
 agreeable offering, atoning for the Land and determining the
 judgement of wickedness, and there shal be no more iniquity. When
they have been confirmed for two years in perfection of way in the
 Foundation of the Community, they shal be set apart as holy within the
 Council of the men of the Community. And the Interpreter shal not
 conceal from them, out of fear of the spirit of apostasy, any of those
 things hidden from Israel which have been discovered by him.
 And when these become members of the Community in Israel
 according to al these rules, they shal separate from the habitation of
 unjust men and shal go into the wilderness to prepare there the way of
 Him; as it is written, Prepare in the wilderness the way of . , make
 straight in the desert a path for our God (Isa. xl, 3). This (path) is the
 study of the Law which He commanded by the hand of Moses, that they
 may do according to al that has been revealed from age to age, and
 as the Prophets have revealed by His Holy Spirit.
 And no man among the members of the Covenant of the Community
 who deliberately, on any point whatever, turns aside from al that is
 commanded, shal touch the pure Meal of the men of holiness or know
 anything of their counsel until his deeds are purified from al injustice
 and he walks in perfection of way. And then, according to the
 judgement of the Congregation, he shal be admitted to the Council
 and shal be inscribed in his rank. This rule shal apply to whoever
 enters the Community.
 And these are the rules which the men of perfect holiness shall
 follow in their commerce with one another
 Every man who enters the Council of Holiness, (the Council of those)
 who walk in the way of perfection as commanded by God, and who
 deliberately or through negligence transgresses one word of the Law
 of Moses, on any point whatever, shal be expeled from the Council of
 the Community and shal return no more; no man of holiness shal be
 associated in his property or counsel in any matter at al. But if he has
 acted inadvertently, he shal be excluded from the pure Meal and the
 Council and they shal interpret the rule (as folows). For two years he
 shal take no part in judgement or ask for counsel; but if, during that
 time, his way becomes perfect, {then he shal return) (4Q258) to the
(Court of) Inquiry and the Council, in accordance with the judgement of
 the Congregation, provided that he commit no further inadvertent sin
 during two ful years. IX For one sin of inadvertence (alone) he shal do
 penance for two years. But as for him who has sinned deliberately, he
 shal never return; only the man who has sinned inadvertently shal be
 tried for two years, that his way and counsel may be made perfect
 according to the judgement of the Congregation. And afterwards, he
 shal be inscribed in his rank in the Community of Holiness.
 When these become members of the Community in Israel according
 to al these rules, they shal establish the spirit of holiness according to
 everlasting truth. They shal atone for guilty rebelion and for sins of
 unfaithfulness, that they may obtain loving-kindness for the Land
 without the flesh of holocausts and the fat of sacrifice. And prayer
 rightly offered sha l be as an acceptable fragrance of righteousness,
 and perfection of way as a delectable free-wi l offering. At that time, the
 men of the Community shal set apart a House of Holiness in order that
 it may be united to the most holy things and a House of Community for
 Israel, for those who walk in perfection. The sons of Aaron alone shal
 command in matters of justice and property, and every rule concerning
 the men of the Community shal be determined according to their word.
 As for the property of the men of holiness who walk in perfection, it
 shal not be merged with that of the men of injustice who have not
 purified their life by separating themselves from iniquity and walking in
 the way of perfection. They shal depart from none of the counsels of
 the Law to walk in al the stubbornness of their hearts, but shal be ruled
 by the primitive precepts in which the men of the Community were first
 instructed until there shal come the Prophet and the Messiahs of
 Aaron and Israel.
 These are the precepts in which the Master shall walk in His
 commerce with all the living, according to the rule proper to
 every season and according to the worth of every man
 He shal do the wil of God according to al that has been revealed
 from age to age.
He shal measure out al knowledge discovered throughout the ages,
 together with the Precept of the age.
 He shal separate and weigh the sons of righteousness according to
 their spirit.
 He shal hold firmly to the elect of the time according to His wi l, as
 He has commanded.
 He shal judge every man according to his spirit. He shal admit him
 in accordance with the cleanness of his hands and advance him in
 accordance with his understanding. And he shal love and hate
 likewise.
 He shal not rebuke the men of the Pit nor dispute with them.
 He shal conceal the teaching of the Law from men of injustice, but
 shal impart true knowledge and righteous judgement to those who
 have chosen the Way. He shal guide them al in knowledge according
 to the spirit of each and according to the rule of the age, and shal thus
 instruct them in the mysteries of marvelous truth, so that in the midst of
 the men of the Community they may walk perfectly together in al that
 has been revealed to them. This is the time for the preparation of the
 way into the wilderness, and he shal teach them to do al that is
 required at that time and to separate from al those who have not
 turned aside from al injustice.
 These are the rules of conduct for the Master in those times with
 respect to His loving and hating
 Everlasting hatred in a spirit of secrecy for the men of perdition! He
 shal leave to them wealth and earnings like a slave to his lord and like
 a poor man to his master.
 He shal be a man zealous for the Precept whose time is for the Day
 of Revenge. He shal perform the wil of God in al his deeds, and in al
 his dominion as He has commanded. He shal freely delight in al that
 befa ls him and nothing shal please him save God’s wil. He shal
 delight in al the words of His mouth and shal desire nothing except
 His command. He shal watch always [for] the judgement of God, and
 shal bless his Maker [for al His goodness] and declare [His mercies]
in al that befals.
 He shal bless Him [with the offering] of the lips X at the times
 ordained by Him: at the beginning of the dominion of light, and at its
 end when it retires to its appointed place; at the beginning of the
 watches of darkness when He unlocks their storehouse and spreads
 them out, and also at their end when they retire before the light; when
 the heavenly lights shine out from the dweling-place of Holiness, and
 also when they retire to the place of Glory; at the entry of the (monthly)
 seasons on the days of the new moon, and also at their end when they
 succeed to one another. Their renewal is a great day for the Holy of
 Holies, and a sign for the unlocking of everlasting mercies at the
 beginning of seasons in al times to come.
 At the beginning of the months of the (yearly) seasons 
and on the holy days appointed for remembrance, 
in their seasons I wi l bless Him 
with the offering of the lips 
according to the Precept engraved for ever: 
at the beginning of the years 
and at the end of their seasons 
when their appointed law is fulfi led, 
on the day decreed by Him 
that they should pass from one to the other
the season of early harvest to the summer time, 
the season of sowing to the season of grass, 
the seasons of years to their weeks (of years)
and at the beginning of their weeks 
for the season of Jubilee. 
Al my life the engraved Precept shal be on my tongue 
as the fruit of praise 
and the portion of my lips.
 I wi l sing with knowledge and al my music 
shal be for the glory of God. 
(My) lyre (and) my harp shal sound 
for His holy order 
and I wi l tune the pipe of my lips 
to His right measure. 
With the coming of day and night 
I wi l enter the Covenant of God, 
and when evening and morning depart 
I wi l recite His decrees. 
I wi l place in them my bounds without return.
 I wi l declare His judgement concerning my sins, 
and my transgressions shal be before my eyes 
as an engraved Precept. 
I wi l say to God, ‘My Righteousness’ 
and ‘Author of my Goodness’ to the Most High, 
‘Fountain of Knowledge’ and ‘Source of Holiness’, 
‘Summit of Glory’ and ‘Almighty Eternal Majesty’. 
I wi l choose that which He teaches me 
and wil delight in His judgement of me.
 Before I move my hands and feet 
I wi l bless His Name. 
I wi l praise Him before I go out or enter, 
or sit or rise, 
and whilst I lie on the couch of my bed. 
I wi l bless Him with the offering 
of that which proceeds from my lips 
from the midst of the ranks of men, 
and before I lift my hands to eat 
of the pleasant fruits of the earth. 
I wi l bless Him for His exceeding wonderful deeds 
at the beginning of fear and dread 
and in the abode of distress and desolation. 
I wi l meditate on His power 
and wil lean on His mercies al day long. 
I know that judgement of al the living 
is in His hand, 
and that al His deeds are truth. 
I wi l praise Him when distress is unleashed 
and wil magnify Him also because of His salvation.
 I wi l pay to no man the reward of evil; 
I wi l pursue him with goodness. 
For judgement of al the living is with God 
and it is He who wil render to man his reward. 
I wi l not envy in a spirit of wickedness, 
my soul shal not desire the riches of violence. 
I wi l not grapple with the men of perdition 
until the Day of Revenge, 
but my wrath shal not turn from the men of falsehood 
and I wi l not rejoice until judgement is made. 
I wi l bear no rancour 
against them that turn from transgression, 
but wi l have no pity 
on al who depart from the way. 
I wi l offer no comfort to the smitten 
until their way becomes perfect.
 I wi l not keep Belial within my heart, 
and in my mouth shal be heard 
no foly or sinful deceit, 
no cunning or lies shal be found on my lips. 
The fruit of holiness sha l be on my tongue 
and no abominations shal be found upon it. 
I wi l open my mouth 
in songs of thanksgiving, 
and my tongue shal always proclaim 
the goodness of God and the sin of men 
until their transgression ends. 
I wi l cause vanities 
to cease from my lips, 
uncleanness and crookedness 
from the knowledge of my heart.
 I wi l impart/conceal knowledge with discretion 
and wil prudently hedge it within a firm bound 
to preserve faith and strong judgement 
in accordance with the justice of God. 
I wi l distribute the Precept 
by the measuring-cord of the times, 
and ... righteousness 
and loving-kindness towards the oppressed, 
encouragement to the troubled heart 
XI and discernment to the erring spirit, 
teaching understanding to them that murmur 
that they may answer meekly 
before the haughty of spirit 
and humbly before men of injustice 
who point the finger and speak of iniquity 
and who are zealous for wealth.
 As for me, 
my justification is with God. 
In His hand are the perfection of my way 
and the uprightness of my heart. 
He wil wipe out my transgression 
through His righteousness.
 For my light has sprung 
from the source of His knowledge; 
my eyes have beheld His marvelous deeds, 
and the light of my heart, the mystery to come. 
He that is everlasting 
is the support of my right hand; 
the way of my steps is over stout rock 
which nothing shal shake; 
for the rock of my steps is the truth of God 
and His might is the support of my right hand.
 From the source of His righteousness 
is my justification, 
and from His marvelous mysteries 
is the light in my heart. 
My eyes have gazed 
on that which is eternal, 
on wisdom concealed from men, 
on knowledge and wise design 
(hidden) from the sons of men; 
on a fountain of righteousness 
and on a storehouse of power, 
on a spring of glory 
(hidden) from the assembly of flesh. 
God has given them to His chosen ones 
as an everlasting possession, 
and has caused them to inherit 
the lot of the Holy Ones. 
He has joined their assembly 
to the Sons of Heaven 
to be a Council of the Community, 
a foundation of the Building of Holiness, 
and eternal Plantation throughout a l ages to come.
 As for me, 
I belong to wicked mankind, 
to the company of unjust flesh. 
My iniquities, rebe lions, and sins, 
together with the perversity of my heart, 
belong to the company of worms 
and to those who walk in darkness. 
For mankind has no way, 
and man is unable to establish his steps 
since justification is with God 
and perfection of way is out of His hand. 
Al things come to pass by His knowledge; 
He establishes al things by His design 
and without Him nothing is done.
As for me, 
if I stumble, the mercies of God 
shal be my eternal salvation. 
If I stagger because of the sin of flesh, 
my justification sha l be 
by the righteousness of God which endures for ever. 
When my distress is unleashed 
He wil deliver my soul from the Pit 
and wil direct my steps to the way. 
He wil draw me near by His grace, 
and by His mercy wil He bring my justification. 
He wil judge me in the righteousness of His truth 
and in the greatness of His goodness 
He wil pardon al my sins. 
Through His righteousness he wi l cleanse me 
of the uncleanness of man 
and of the sins of the children of men, 
that I may confess to God His righteousness, 
and His majesty to the Most High.
 Blessed art Thou, my God, 
who openest the heart of Thy servant to knowledge! 
Establish a l his deeds in righteousness, 
and as it pleases Thee to do for the elect of mankind, 
grant that the son of Thy handmaid 
may stand before Thee for ever.
 For without Thee no way is perfect, 
and without Thy wi l nothing is done. 
It is Thou who hast taught al knowledge 
and al things come to pass by Thy wil. 
There is none beside Thee to dispute Thy counsel 
or to understand al Thy holy design, 
or to contemplate the depth of Thy mysteries 
and the power of Thy might.
Who can endure Thy glory, 
and what is the son of man 
in the midst of Thy wonderful deeds? 
What shal one born of woman 
be accounted before Thee? 
Kneaded from the dust, 
his abode is the nourishment of worms. 
He is but a shape, but moulded clay, 
and inclines towards dust. 
What shal hand-moulded clay reply? 
What counsel shal it understand?
Community Rule manuscripts from Cave 4
 4QSd=4Q258
 This‘is the best preserved of the ten 4QS manuscripts. Seven columns
 of the text have survived, five of them containing ful-length lines.
 Column 1, with a wide margin on the right, is almost certainly the
 beginning of the Scrol. It corresponds to 1QS v, 1. The last identifiable
 passage represents 1QS XI, 7. The word ‘God’ (‘el) is written twice
 with palaeo-Hebrew letters (at 1QS IX, 25 and x, 9). Columns I and I of
 Sd (1QS v, I-VI, 7) provide a shorter and smoother version of the Rule
 than 1QS. The more fragmentary 4QSb (=4Q256) supports the present
 version on the essential points. The most significant peculiarities of
 4QSd (=4Q258) are the almost complete absence of the ‘ful’ speling
 characteristic of the Qumran sectarian manuscripts, the different
 opening line and a repeated failure to mention ‘the Priests, the Sons of
 Zadok’. For both its occurrences in 1QS, 4QSd (and 4QSb) read ‘the
 Congregation’ (ha-rabbim), an alternative reading likely to possess
 important historical implications.
 For the editio princeps, see P. S. Alexander and G. Vermes, DJD,
 XXVI, 83-128. See also G. Vermes, ‘The Leadership of the Qumran
 Community: Sons of Zadok - Priests - Congregation’, in the Martin
 Hengel Festschrift Geschichte-Tradition-Reflexion, ed. P. Schäfer
 (Tübingen, 1996), 375-84.
 I (=1QS v, I-20) Teaching for the Master concerning the men of the Law
 (or: the Master who is the superior of the men of the Law) who have
 freely pledged themselves to convert from al evil and hold fast to al
 that He has commanded. And they shal separate from the
 congregation of the men of injustice and shal unite with respect to
 doctrine and property, and they shal be under the authority of the
Congregation concerning al matters of doctrine and property. They
 shal practise humility and righteousness and justice and loving
[kindness] and modesty in al their ways. And no man shal walk in the
 stubbornness of his heart so as to stray. He is rather to lay [a
 foundation] of truth for Israel for the Community, for a l those who have
 freely pledged themselves to Holiness in Aaron and to a House of
 Truth in Israel and for those who jo[in th]em for a Community. Whoever
 enters the Council [of the Commu]nity shal undertake by binding (oath)
 to [return t]o the Law of Moses with al (his) heart and soul, to al that
 has been revealed from the L[aw].
 [And whoever enters] the Council of the me[n] of the Communi[ty
 shal separate from al the men] of injustice... He shal not touch the
 purity of the men [of holine]ss and shal not eat with (them) [in
 community. And no] one of the men of the Community [shal folow] their
 decision in any [doctrine] and judgement. And ... of work. No one from
 the men of holiness shal eat ... [And] they shal not lean upon [work]s of
 vanity, for they are al vanity who [do not know His Covenant and al
 who despis]e His word He wil blot them out from the world. Al their
 deeds are defileme[nt] be[fore Him and al]l [their property unclean.] ...
 Gentiles(?) and they pronounce oaths and execrations and vows. [But
 when a man enters the Covenant according to al these precepts, that
 he may be joined to the h]oly [Congregation,] they [shal examine their
 spirit 
in community,] am[ong themselves] con[cerning their
 understanding] I (1QS v, 21-VI, 7) and their practice of the Law under
 the authority of the sons of Aaron who have freely pledged themselves
 to restore His Covenant and heed to al the precepts commanded by
 Him to be practised by the multitude of Israel who have freely pledged
 themselves to return in common. They shal be inscribed in the order,
 one after another, each according to his understanding and his deeds
 in the Law, that al may obey one another, the man of lesser rank the
 greater. And they shal examine their spirit and their deeds in the Law
 yearly so that each man may be advanced in accordance with [his]
 un[derstanding] or moved down in accordance with his aberrations.
 They shal rebuke one another (in) loving-kindness. Let no man
 address his companion with anger or il-temper or wicked envy. Also
 let no man accuse his companion before the Congregation without
having rebuked him before witnesses. These are the ways in which al
 of them shal walk, each man with his companion, wherever they dwel.
 [The man of lesser rank] shal ob[ey] the greater in matters of work and
 pro[perty. And they shal e]at [in common] and bless in common and
 delibera[te] in common. [And wherever there are ten] I (1QS VI, 7-10)
 men of [the Council of the Community, there shal not lack] a pri[est
 from a]mong them. [And] they [shal sit, each m]an according to his
 rank, [before him and shal be asked their counsel in al things in that

 bless the firstfruit of the bread] and of the wine. [And where the ten are,
 there shal never lack a man among them who shal study the Law day
 and night. And the Congregation shal w]atch [for one third of every
 night of the year, to read the Book] IV . V (IS VI, 13) ... [and whoever
 draws] his hand from be[neath his garment] ... VI (1QS V I, 6-21) ...
 [and] to pay to the wicked [their reward. It shal be a tried wal, that
 precious corner-stone whose foundations shal not rock nor sway from]

 [They shal be an acceptance to atone for the land and to determine

4QSe=4Q259
 Three fragmentary columns of a leather scrol contain damaged
 sections of 1QS VI-IX. The text translated comes from columns I and I
 and represents an important doctrinal section of 1QS (V I, 4-IX, I) in an
 abridged form. Not only are some of the interlinear additions to 1QS
 absent, suggesting their later editorial nature, but 4QSe (4Q259) jumps
 from 1QS V I, 15 to IX, 12, thus omitting among other things the
 mention of the ‘Prophet and the Messiahs of Aaron and Israel’ (1QS
 IX, I). It would seem that the copyist of this manuscript substituted
 4Q319 (the calendric document of Otot) for the text corresponding to
 1QS X-XI.
 For the editio princeps, see P. S. Alexander and G. Vermes, DJD,
 XXVI, 129-52.

Entry into the Covenant
 (4Q275)
 Previously caled 4QTohorot Ba, this tiny fragment represents a
 document describing the entry into the Covenant, known from the
 Community Rule (1QS), and aludes to a festival in the third month, i.e.
 the Feast of Weeks of Pentecost, when according to one of the Cave
 4 manuscripts of the Damascus Document (4Q266) the Qumran
 Covenant renewal took place.
 For the editio princeps, see P. S. Alexander and G. Vermes, DJD,
 XXVI, 200—16.

 shal enter by genealogy... And the Guardian shal [curse (the
 unrepentant), saying ‘Be damned without] mercy. [Let him be cur]sed
 ...’ And he wi l remove him] from his inheritance for ev[er] ... when he
 visits destruction ...
Four Classes of the Community
 (4Q279)
 Formerly known as 4QTohorot Da, this fragment is one of three smal
 scraps which have partly preserved the division of the Community into
 four lots or classes, already known from CD XIV, 5-6, viz. Priests,
 Levites, Israelites and Proselytes.
 For the editio princeps, see P. S. Alexander and G. Vermes, DJD,
 XXVI, 217-23.

 second lot to the Levites ranked in order] each according to his spirit.

 according to his spirit. And] the fourth lot wi l belong to the Prosely[tes]
